article_title: PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1 Lands With Parallel Autovacuum, Self-Scaling Async I/O, and a Planner-Hint Extension

content_preview.title: PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1 Lands With Parallel Autovacuum, Self-Scaling Async I/O, and a Planner-Hint Extension
content_preview.summary: PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1, released June 4, brings parallel autovacuum, self-scaling async I/O workers, a new pg_plan_advice planner extension, SQL/PGQ graph queries, and turns JIT off by default. GA is targeted for September/October 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The PostgreSQL Global Development Group released the first beta of PostgreSQL 19 on June 4, 2026, opening the testing phase for the next major version of the open-source relational database, [according to the project's release announcement](https://www.postgresql.org/about/news/postgresql-19-beta-1-released-3313/). The release builds on the asynchronous I/O subsystem introduced in PostgreSQL 18 and adds parallel autovacuum, a new planner-control extension, SQL-standard property grap...

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough, well-organized release News piece (832 words) covering PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1 across performance, planner, replication, security, observability, and changed defaults — appropriately framed as beta, not production-ready.
editor_notes.source_verification: Both snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (official PostgreSQL announcement) confirms VERBATIM every technical specific: June 4 2026, the September/October GA window, io_method=worker, io_min_workers, io_max_workers, autovacuum_max_parallel_workers, REPACK / CONCURRENTLY, the '2x better performance on inserts', pg_plan_advice, pg_stash_advice, SQL/PGQ, MERGE/SPLIT PARTITIONS, GROUP BY ALL, WAIT FOR LSN, sequence replication, wal_level, pg_hosts.conf, md5_password_warnings, pg_stat_lock, pg_stat_recovery, EXPLAIN ANALYZE, 'Just-in-time compilation (JIT) is now disabled by default', default_toast_compression / lz4, the RADIUS removal, and online checksums. source-1 (Linux Compatible) confirms the 'after years of mixed results in production environments' JIT quote verbatim, the up-to-double FK-insert throughput, and the RADIUS removal.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Exemplary primary-source discipline (Rule 9): every config GUC, command, and feature name is cited to and verbatim in the official announcement — zero fabricated identifiers, which is the dominant failure mode for release coverage. The 'breaking change' characterization of the RADIUS removal is a fair paraphrase of Linux Compatible's 'drops RADIUS authentication entirely'. The internal cross-reference to the May PostgreSQL security release was verified to exist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' correctly flags the beta status and the un-benchmarked performance claims.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct from the May PostgreSQL security-release coverage it cross-references.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified release submission with model primary-source citation. APPROVE.
